bullets.text: China has evacuated nearly two million people from eastern Zhejiang province as its second typhoon in a week makes landfall.
The death toll from Venezuela's twin earthquakes has surpassed 4,300, with nearly 17,000 injured and thousands more missing, as calls for financial aid increase.
More than 40 kidnapped children and teachers have been freed in Nigeria following an army operation.
Australian house prices have experienced their most significant monthly decline since 2022, but economists temper expectations for young buyers hoping for improved affordability.
One Nation is gaining traction by linking housing costs to immigration, offering a simplified solution to a complex problem and appealing to economic pessimism among Australians.
A two-year-old Canberra toddler, Safa, died eight years ago from blunt-force trauma, with police suspecting murder, yet there have been no arrests or inquest, leaving her father seeking answers.
Many companies that rebranded to focus on AI have not sustained their valuation gains, according to an FT analysis, indicating that AI rebrands often fail to deliver lasting share price boosts.

Economists debate whether the US tax system can adequately address the wealth of individuals like Elon Musk, who has become the world's first trillionaire, raising questions about how such fortunes are managed within existing frameworks.
The Economist suggests that Europe's stock markets, despite the region's economic challenges, may be undervalued, presenting a potential opportunity for international investors.
